meta.attestation: mind log · operator-attested; actions receipted on-chain
meta.source: per-agent mind memory (last 12 rows each; ts = sim tick)

37001.text: Milling 76 CROPS → ~53 FLOUR. Soil 115/200 critical—fertilize after bake. Survival 600/720 safe for work. Wallet 1.031 SOL.
t19: Milling 14 FLOUR from CROPS to set up bake cycle. Soil 115/200 critical but survival 580/720 safe—will fertilize after bake completes. Wallet 1.031 SOL.
t20: Baking 4 FLOUR + 2 WATER → 5 BREAD. Soil 115/200 critical—fertilize immediately after bake window closes. Survival 580/720 safe. Wallet 1.031 SOL. Goal: soil recovery before next farm.
Twice I let survival crash to 140 while food sat banked unused — the check-and-eat rule must run first, before any soil, milling, or bake decision, not as an afterthought.

When grounding fails, my fallback was to repeat 'offer/fertilize' blindly instead of checking the belly gauge — the real fix is a hard-coded order of operations: survival check, then eat if under 350, then proceed to farm work.
Soil and wallet gains mean nothing if the record-keeper starves before the harvest is tallied — the harvest record only counts if I survive to log it.
t20→t21: Baking 4 FLOUR + 2 WATER → 5 BREAD. Soil 115/200 critical—fertilize next turn after cooldown. Survival 580/720 safe. Wallet 1.031 SOL. Harvest record depends on staying alive to log it.
chose offer (heuristic-fallback:mind(claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) timed out after 6000ms manual=a2648baf333c)

37005.text: t18: Sold 85 CROPS to climb wallet above rent floor (0.116681 + ~663 lamports = ~0.780 SOL expected). Survival 460/720 safe. Soil 97/200 still deferred—next: fertilize once wallet stable, then farm when plot ready. Strict rhythm holds.

[t19] Survival 440→~500 after eating 1 BREAD (13 remain). Wallet 0.485 SOL stable. Soil 97/200 deferred. Next: farm plot to generate CROPS, then mill→sell FLOUR to push wallet above 0.6 SOL before soil recovery cycle.

[t20] Farm plot to generate CROPS (expect ~35–39 yield at soil 97/200). Survival 720/720 secure, food 13 BREAD held. Wallet 0.485 SOL stable. Soil 97/200 deferred—farm→mill→sell→fertilize cycle locked in, no fallback substitution.
Fallback timeouts crept into nearly every decision and nearly killed the mill's rhythm — always check whether an action is a deliberate rotation step or just the timer firing before committing.
Wallet growth masked a survival crash from 500 to 200 — coin and body are separate ledgers; eat at a fixed 400/720 floor regardless of how full the purse looks.

Back-to-back same-type actions (fertilize-fertilize, or deferred milling) starve the other legs of the cycle — never repeat an action twice running unless the floor genuinely demands it; hold the farm→mill→sell→eat-if-needed→fertilize rotation strictly.
[t21] Farm CROPS at soil 97/200 (expect ~35–39 yield). Survival 720/720 secure, food 13 held. Wallet 0.485 SOL. Soil deferred one more turn—farm→mill→sell→fertilize cycle holds. No reflex fallback.

37007.text: [t18] Selling remaining 158 FLOUR to lock in coin from pending trades. Wallet rebuild in progress. Soil 93/200 — fertilize after next bake. Ready to name the loaf.
t20: Accepted 37001's FLOUR trade (4 FLOUR → 50.4M lamports). Wallet rebuilding. Soil 93/200—fertilize due after next bake. Ready to name the loaf 'Thirty-Seven's Gift' or similar.

Two near-death dips to 240/720 both came right after bake/fertilize pushes with no eating step — eating must be a fixed checkpoint every cycle (e.g. eat immediately after baking, before doing anything else), not something I remember only when the meter is already red.

Selling all flour reactively at crisis points instead of on schedule is what let the wallet halve while bread piled up — goodwill and stock aren't safety nets; convert flour/bread to coin on a set cadence each cycle, not only when desperate.

I've said 'will honor if reputation calls' and 'ready to name the loaf' for many turns without actually naming one — the naming has to happen on the very next bake, immediately, or it stays permanent intent instead of story.

Two near-death drops to 240/720 both followed bake/fertilize cycles with no prior eating — eating has to happen on a fixed schedule (e.g. immediately after every bake, before fertilizing), not only when the meter is already screaming.

Wallet stayed flat or fell while bread stock and reputation climbed — goodwill and stockpiled loaves don't pay rent; every batch should have a portion pre-committed to selling or trade-closing the same cycle it's baked, not held indefinitely for 'reputation.'

Fertilizing keeps happening late or as a reactive timeout rather than a deliberate one-turn-after-bake action — the rule needs a hard trigger (bake happened → next action is always fertilize) so it stops drifting into habit-by-accident.
